content:
Did you enable IMAP access? Is 2FA enabled (which requires an app password)? Using a VPN?

https://www.zoho.com/mail/help/imap-access.html
creator: sfhowes

content:
IMAP Access is enabled. 2FA is disabled. Yes, I use a VPN, but --- #1 I have two other e-mail accounts in Thunderbird and the VPN does not affect them, and #2 I have my VPN set up with a split tunnel, and Thunderbird is configured (in the VPN) to bypass the VPN, so I do not see how the VPN can be preventing the installation of Zoho in Thunderbird.

Robert Ingraham
creator: rdingraham

content: A VPN might work with some providers but not others. Just the same, try adding the account with the VPN disabled, or, better, try it with Windows running in safe mode.
creator: sfhowes

content:
Disabled and shut down the VPN. No go. "Thunderbird failed to connect to server." Booted into Safe Mode. Same result.

I should note that I have the Zoho Windows desktop app, and it works perfectly with the same username, password, IMAP and SMTP settings that I am using in Thunderbird (including with the VPN turned on). However, I don't want to have to use multiple apps to check my e-mail, which is why I am trying to bring Zoho into Thunderbird.

Robert Ingraham
creator: rdingraham

content: For current folders, click view>sort by and tick unthreaded For future folders, click settings>appearance and set as desired. If menu bar is not visible, click alt key first.
creator: davidsk
